forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system in the Tasman Sea is slowly moving east. A trough of low pressure lies across the northeast and will remain near stationary. A cold front will move east across the Southern Ocean over the weekend, with the trough also starting to move east ahead of it. A second cold front will follow early next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers across central and southern pastoral districts, increasing across eastern and southeastern districts during the afternoon and evening. Possible thunderstorms about the North East Pastoral and West Coast districts, more likely across the Eyre Peninsula. Maximum temperatures near to above average. Northeast to northwesterly winds.
An isolated shower or two and possible thunderstorm about the Mount Lofty Ranges and Mid North, easing this evening. East to northeasterly winds.

Showers and thunderstorms in the east, easing during the morning before redeveloping about central and northern districts during the afternoon. Some thunderstorms may become severe, particularly across the Pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, ahead of a west to southwesterly change in the west, reaching the Eyre Peninsula in the evening.
Isolated showers about the agricultural areas and the far north. Possible thunderstorms in the east and north. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

text.p: Isolated showers about the far north. Showers developing about the agricultural areas during the afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds, tending west to northwesterly about the south during the day.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Saturday: 1 to 5 mm about the west, increasing to 5 to 15 mm east of around Coober Pedy, with isolated falls up to 40 mm possible with thunderstorms.
